---
title: "OPS+ and BABIP Analysis"
author: ""
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: dark
    slide-number: true
    transition: slide
    background-transition: fade
    css: styles.css
execute:
  echo: true
---

# ⚾ OPS+ & BABIP

## A Data Story in Baseball

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Why This Matters

-   OPS+ captures overall offensive value
-   BABIP reflects outcomes on balls in play
-   Together: signal vs. noise in hitting performance

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Key Question

> How much of offensive performance is skill vs. luck?

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Dataset Overview

-   League: MLB
-   Variables:
    -   OPS+
    -   BABIP
-   Goal: Understand relationship and variability

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## OPS+ Explained

-   Adjusted for park and league

-   100 = league average

-   

    > 100 = above average hitter

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## BABIP Explained

-   Batting Average on Balls In Play
-   Influenced by:
    -   Defense\
    -   Luck\
    -   Contact quality

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Hypothesis

-   Higher BABIP → Higher OPS+
-   But not perfectly (luck plays a role)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Visualization

```{r}
# (Paste your original plotting code here)
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## What We See

-   Positive relationship\
-   Considerable spread\
-   Outliers matter

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Interpretation

-   Some players sustain high BABIP (skill)\
-   Others regress (luck)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Takeaways

-   OPS+ is more stable\
-   BABIP is more volatile\
-   Combining both gives better insight

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Final Thought

> Not all hits are created equal ⚾

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Thank You

Questions?

------------------------------------------------------------------------

```{=html}
<style>
.reveal {
  background-image: url('https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1508341591423-4347099e1f19');
  background-size: cover;
  background-position: center;
}

.reveal h1, .reveal h2, .reveal h3 {
  color: #ffffff;
  text-shadow: 2px 2px 4px #000000;
}

.reveal section {
  background-color: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.6);
  border-radius: 15px;
  padding: 20px;
}
</style>
```

Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished presentation. To learn more about Quarto presentations see <https://quarto.org/docs/presentations/>.

## Bullets

When you click the **Render** button a document will be generated that includes:

-   Content authored with markdown
-   Output from executable code

## Code

When you click the **Render** button a presentation will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this:

```{r}
1 + 1
```
